steps.title: Identify the two verb phrases and the structural requirement
steps.content: The sentence contains two verbs about Udé: “referred to X as Y” and “honors its legacy”. They must be connected grammatically without creating a comma splice—two independent clauses separated by only a comma.

steps.title: Apply the participial phrase rule
steps.content:
Choice D restructures the first verb as a participial phrase (“having referred”), making “honors” the main verb.

When a participial phrase interrupts the main clause—positioning itself between the subject and main verb—it must be enclosed in commas.

The correct structure is: Udé, having referred..., honors. A and C create comma splices (joining two independent clauses with only a comma), and B misplaces a comma between the subject and verb.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
